notes: MVP was Gmail-only; post-MVP phases may extend sources while preserving the same canonical vacancy pipeline.
Postgres is the source of truth.
Telegram MVP UX is digest plus review queue.
One primary AI provider in MVP.
GitHub metadata is mirrored via labels where project fields are not yet set.
Backlog structure (epics, tasks, labels, workflow) is locked; changes require approval and JSON + mirror sync.

labels.name: human-only
labels.description: Must be done by a human

epics.title: Foundation and Backlog Control
epics.summary: Create the canonical backlog structure, import process, and issue body contract for AI-assisted delivery.

tasks.title: Lock canonical backlog structure for Linear and GitHub mirror
tasks.goal: Freeze the backlog taxonomy, metadata model, and mirroring rules.
acceptance: All epics exist in one canonical file

tasks.title: Define AI backlog ticket body format
tasks.goal: Standardize issue bodies for AI-generated tickets.
acceptance: Template contains goal, inputs, outputs, acceptance, AI generation, human verification

tasks.title: Backlog hygiene and synchronization rules
tasks.goal: Define who updates Linear, when GitHub mirror is updated, and how drift is handled.

tasks.title: Approve backlog taxonomy and phase boundaries
tasks.goal: Get user sign-off on epics, labels, and phase structure.

epics.title: Access, Secrets, and Bootstrap
epics.summary: Repair access paths and define the pragmatic local-to-hosted secret handling flow under the roleforge keyring namespace.

tasks.title: Repair GitHub auth and confirm project scope
tasks.goal: Restore valid gh access with project scope so GitHub placement can run.
outputs: Valid gh auth with project scope

tasks.title: Define Linear access path and write method
tasks.goal: Decide whether Linear placement will be API-driven or manual-import-first.
acceptance: Linear token or manual placement path exists

tasks.title: Define roleforge keyring namespace contract
tasks.goal: Create a stable secret naming scheme under service=roleforge.
acceptance: Domains are defined for google, telegram, openai, anthropic, db, app

tasks.title: Document bootstrap sequence for Gmail, Telegram, and AI keys
tasks.goal: Define the order in which required credentials are created and stored.
acceptance: Sequence covers Google Cloud project, Gmail OAuth, Telegram bot, one AI provider

tasks.title: Create Google Cloud project and complete Gmail OAuth bootstrap
tasks.goal: Get Gmail OAuth client and refresh token ready for local keyring import.

tasks.title: Create Telegram bot and choose primary AI provider
tasks.goal: Finish remaining external bootstrap actions required before runtime work.
acceptance: Primary provider is chosen and documented

epics.title: Gmail Intake
epics.summary: Implement the only ingestion path in MVP: Gmail API polling scoped by a dedicated label and backed by DB idempotency.

tasks.title: Define Gmail-only intake spec and scoping rules
tasks.goal: Freeze label-first intake behavior, polling cadence, and whole-inbox exclusions.
acceptance: No watch/history hot path in MVP

tasks.title: Implement gmail_reader around messages.list and messages.get
tasks.goal: Fetch Gmail message IDs from the intake label and hydrate new messages only.
acceptance: Only new gmail_message_id values are processed
human_should_verify: OAuth and label behavior on real mailbox

tasks.title: Persist raw Gmail metadata and body variants
tasks.goal: Store the source payload needed for audit and replay without inventing extra connectors.
acceptance: HTML and text variants are stored
human_should_verify: Stored data is enough but not excessive

tasks.title: Capture Gmail failure and retry policy
tasks.goal: Define transient retry behavior and permanent failure handling for Gmail reads.
acceptance: Permanent auth failures surface as admin alerts

tasks.title: Choose final Gmail label and mailbox strategy
tasks.goal: Lock whether MVP uses a dedicated Gmail mailbox or a dedicated label inside an existing mailbox.
acceptance: Mailbox or label naming is approved

epics.title: Parsing, Normalization, and Dedup
epics.summary: Turn Gmail messages into normalized vacancies using deterministic extraction first and global dedup second.

tasks.title: Define parser behavior for single alerts and multi-job digests
tasks.goal: Freeze the parsing contract for the two major Gmail source shapes in MVP.
acceptance: Single-job and digest behaviors are explicit
Parser does not require LLM first-pass

tasks.title: Implement deterministic extraction pipeline
tasks.goal: Extract vacancy candidates from Gmail messages before any LLM usage.
acceptance: Deterministic extraction works on core fixtures
Parse failures are explicit and replayable

tasks.title: Define normalized Gmail-originated vacancy schema
tasks.goal: Create a strict normalized schema for vacancies extracted from Gmail.
acceptance: URL, company, title, location, salary, and parse confidence are represented

tasks.title: Implement URL, title, company, and location normalization
tasks.goal: Normalize the core fields needed for dedup and scoring.
acceptance: Canonical fields are stable enough for dedup

tasks.title: Define and implement global dedup across messages and digest fragments
tasks.goal: Merge the same vacancy across repeated emails without creating duplicate review items.
acceptance: Canonical vacancy and observation links exist
One vacancy can map to many source fragments

tasks.title: Review parser fixtures against real Gmail samples
tasks.goal: Validate that the fixture set reflects actual Gmail job-alert diversity.
acceptance: Coverage gaps are identified and fixed

epics.title: Profiles and Scoring
epics.summary: Support minimal multiple profiles and one shared scoring formula with profile-specific filters and weights.

tasks.title: Define minimal multi-profile schema for MVP
tasks.goal: Represent multiple profiles without profile explosion or connector duplication.
acceptance: Profiles store hard filters and weights only

tasks.title: Define hard filters, soft scoring dimensions, and thresholds
tasks.goal: Freeze the scoring contract so all relevant vacancies are preserved but ordered by priority.
acceptance: Eligibility is separate from digest highlighting
Priority buckets and review order are explicit

tasks.title: Implement shared scoring engine with per-profile weights
tasks.goal: Score each canonical vacancy against each active profile using one formula.
acceptance: One vacancy can match multiple profiles without duplication

tasks.title: Implement explainability JSON and review ordering
tasks.goal: Make profile matches inspectable and sortable in a deterministic way.

tasks.title: Define initial profiles, hard filters, weights, and minimum thresholds
tasks.goal: Provide the first real search profiles and matching rules.
acceptance: Each active profile has filters, weights, and threshold

epics.title: Telegram Digest and Review Queue
epics.summary: Deliver a compact digest and pull-based review queue that can handle all relevant vacancies without spamming Telegram.

tasks.title: Define Telegram digest and review queue interaction model
tasks.goal: Freeze the MVP Telegram behavior as digest plus pull-based queue only.
acceptance: Digest and queue flows are explicit

tasks.title: Implement digest message formatter grouped by profile
tasks.goal: Create compact digest messages that summarize all relevant work without pushing every vacancy.

tasks.title: Implement review queue cards and callback actions
tasks.goal: Show one vacancy at a time with fast review actions and next-item navigation.
acceptance: Actions exist for Open, Shortlist, Later, Ignore, Applied, Next
Multi-profile matches do not duplicate cards

tasks.title: Log all Telegram sends and review actions
tasks.goal: Persist digest sends, queue sends, and human actions for audit and replay.
acceptance: Every message and callback is recorded

tasks.title: Confirm digest cadence and alert policy
tasks.goal: Lock the initial digest timing and confirm that exceptional instant alerts stay out of MVP.
acceptance: Instant alerts remain deferred unless explicitly approved

epics.title: Postgres Audit Trail and State
epics.summary: Make Postgres the only source of truth for messages, vacancies, matches, deliveries, review actions, and runs.

tasks.title: Implement minimal Postgres MVP schema
tasks.goal: Create the agreed MVP tables and indexes.
acceptance: Profiles, gmail_messages, vacancies, vacancy_observations, profile_matches, telegram_deliveries, review_actions, job_runs exist

tasks.title: Define state transitions and source-of-truth boundaries
tasks.goal: Freeze review states and make Postgres-only boundaries explicit.
acceptance: new, shortlisted, review_later, ignored, applied are defined
No secondary hub exists in MVP

tasks.title: Add uniqueness, idempotency, and replayable source links
tasks.goal: Prevent duplicate ingestion and make reprocessing deterministic.
acceptance: Replay from stored source is possible

tasks.title: Review schema against real operational queries
tasks.goal: Validate that the schema serves real debugging, queue, and audit questions before lock-in.
acceptance: Operator queries can be answered without ad hoc schema changes

epics.title: Reliability, Replay, and Minimal Ops
epics.summary: Add the minimum operational safety net without dragging in heavy queueing or observability platforms.

tasks.title: Implement job_runs logging and execution stats
tasks.goal: Track polling, digest, queue, and replay job outcomes.
acceptance: Every scheduled job records start, finish, status, and summary stats

tasks.title: Define retry policy for Gmail, Telegram, and AI failures
tasks.goal: Set bounded retries and graceful fallback behavior for the three external systems in MVP.
acceptance: No heavy DLQ platform is introduced

tasks.title: Implement replay entrypoints for one message and date window
tasks.goal: Make source reprocessing possible without inventing a full backfill platform.

tasks.title: Define minimal admin alert path
tasks.goal: Route operational failures to one minimal admin notification path.
acceptance: Critical auth or runtime failures create an admin signal

epics.title: Deployment and Runtime
epics.summary: Choose one hosted runtime, define environment contracts, and keep secrets simple from local keyring to hosted secret injection.

tasks.title: Choose hosted runtime and webhook exposure model
tasks.goal: Approve the initial hosting provider and budget envelope for runtime plus database.

tasks.title: Define deployment config and environment contract
tasks.goal: Describe the runtime environment variables, scheduled job endpoints, and webhook contract.
acceptance: Hosted secret injection path is explicit

tasks.title: Define hosted secret mapping from roleforge keyring domains
tasks.goal: Keep local and hosted secret names aligned without introducing extra secret infrastructure.
acceptance: Local roleforge key names map cleanly to hosted secret names

tasks.title: Define Postgres provisioning and backup minimum
tasks.goal: Set the minimum DB runtime requirements without drifting into infra theatre.

epics.title: v2 Enhancements
epics.summary: Expand the working MVP with better profiles, summaries, analytics, and queue ergonomics.

tasks.title: Expand profile configuration beyond MVP minimum
tasks.goal: Support richer multiple-profile behavior after MVP proves itself.
acceptance: Task is refined only after MVP data exists

tasks.title: Improve queue UX, summaries, and analytics
tasks.goal: Bundle post-MVP UX and insight improvements into one refinement track.
acceptance: Not started until MVP is stable

epics.title: v3.1 Feed Expansion
epics.summary: Add RSS and structured feeds only after the Gmail pipeline is stable and measurable.

tasks.title: Define feed registry and kill-switch model
tasks.goal: Prepare the structured-source governance model before adding feeds.
acceptance: Feed work stays blocked until post-MVP

tasks.title: Add feed intake through the same normalized schema and dedup path
tasks.goal: Ensure any future feed work reuses the same pipeline instead of creating a parallel system.
acceptance: Task remains a placeholder until MVP completion

epics.title: v3.2 Official Connector Expansion
epics.summary: Add official APIs and source-specific connectors only when maintenance cost and legal clarity justify them.

tasks.title: Define connector contract for post-MVP source expansion
tasks.goal: Create the selection contract before adding any official connector.
acceptance: Connector work remains blocked until MVP is stable

tasks.title: Refine first official connector candidates
tasks.goal: Validate the first official sources only after the MVP intake loop works.
acceptance: Task is deferred until MVP metrics exist

epics.title: v4 Scoring Engine Enhancement
epics.summary: Replace placeholder scoring dimensions with real keyword- and preference-based matching so delivery and ranking become meaningful.

tasks.title: Implement real keyword overlap in title matching
tasks.goal: Make title matching depend on profile keywords instead of title presence only.
acceptance: Title match uses normalized keyword overlap
Unit tests cover matching and non-matching titles

tasks.title: Implement company preference scoring
tasks.goal: Replace company-present placeholder logic with explicit preferred/excluded company behavior.
acceptance: Excluded companies never produce positive company score

tasks.title: Extend profile config with keywords and skills
tasks.goal: Add keywords, skills, and preferred companies to profile config without new tables.

tasks.title: Implement keyword bonus scoring
tasks.goal: Make keyword bonus depend on actual skills/keyword hits instead of a constant zero.
acceptance: No skills means neutral bonus path

tasks.title: Calibrate score distribution on real data
tasks.goal: Verify that score bands become meaningfully differentiated after scoring changes.
acceptance: High/medium/low bands are no longer collapsed
At least a few real vacancies show materially different scores
human_should_verify: Whether differentiated scores align with operator intuition

tasks.title: Update scoring specification for real matching behavior
tasks.goal: Bring scoring documentation in sync with the real dimension implementations and config fields.

epics.title: v4 Delivery Intelligence
epics.summary: Evolve digest-first delivery into threshold-based alerts and optional micro-batching once scoring is trustworthy.

tasks.title: Add delivery_mode to profiles.config schema
tasks.goal: Model alert and batch thresholds in profile config after the product decision is made.
acceptance: Profile config can express alert and batch settings

tasks.title: Implement roleforge/jobs/alert.py
tasks.goal: Send near-real-time Telegram alerts for newly scored high-signal matches and stay idempotent.
acceptance: Only eligible new matches trigger alerts
Repeated runs do not resend already alerted items
job_runs and telegram_deliveries reflect alert activity

tasks.title: Add alert delivery_type support to telegram_deliveries
tasks.goal: Extend delivery logging so alert messages are first-class audit records.

tasks.title: Implement micro-batch delivery job
tasks.goal: Provide an optional middle path between instant alerts and the digest.
acceptance: Mid-band matches can be batched on a short cadence
Batch path remains low-noise and auditable

tasks.title: Update docs/specs/telegram-interaction.md for alert mode
tasks.goal: Document how digest, queue, alert, and optional micro-batch delivery coexist.

epics.title: v4 AI Enrichment
epics.summary: Add bounded AI enrichment only after deterministic scoring and delivery thresholds are meaningful.

tasks.title: Add ai_metadata JSONB column to vacancies
tasks.goal: Create a bounded storage path for AI summaries, prompt metadata, and enrichment provenance.

tasks.title: Define AI enrichment contract
tasks.goal: Decide which model, prompt, inputs, outputs, and cost envelope are acceptable before runtime AI is added.
acceptance: Input and output shape is explicit
Failure and fallback policy is explicit

tasks.title: Implement roleforge/enrichment.py vacancy summarizer
tasks.goal: Create the vacancy enrichment module once the AI contract is approved.
acceptance: Model is pinned or configurable within contract
Enrichment output is stored in ai_metadata

tasks.title: Add enrichment step to post-scoring pipeline
tasks.goal: Enrich only high-score items and degrade gracefully when AI fails.
acceptance: Only eligible high-score items are enriched
AI failure does not block deterministic pipeline

tasks.title: Add ai_cost_usd to job_runs.summary when enrichment runs
tasks.goal: Expose AI spend from day one of enrichment runtime.

tasks.title: Add prompt versioning pattern for enrichment
tasks.goal: Make enrichment prompts versioned and auditable from the first AI release.
acceptance: Prompt version or hash is stored with outputs

tasks.title: Document AI governance rules in docs/architecture.md
tasks.goal: Define model choice, prompt versioning, and failure behavior rules before AI surface area expands.

epics.title: v4 Scheduler
epics.summary: Introduce a clearer runtime coordination model as the job graph expands beyond Gmail and digest.

tasks.title: Evaluate APScheduler vs schedule vs Postgres-based cron table
tasks.goal: Choose the lightest scheduler that still keeps growing job orchestration understandable.
acceptance: At least three options are compared

tasks.title: Implement roleforge/scheduler.py
tasks.goal: Run all approved jobs from one coordinated entrypoint once the scheduling strategy is chosen.
acceptance: Scheduler can run the approved job set

tasks.title: Document scheduler setup in docs/architecture.md and README.md
tasks.goal: Keep runtime expectations explicit across host and hosted environments.

epics.title: v5 Application Lifecycle
epics.summary: Model applications, employer replies, interviews, and preparation as a first-class workflow on top of the current vacancy pipeline.

tasks.title: Define application schema and write schema/003_applications.sql
tasks.goal: Finalize the state machine and minimum schema for applications, employer threads, and interview events.
acceptance: Core entities and relationships are explicit

tasks.title: Add classified_as column to gmail_messages
tasks.goal: Track whether stored Gmail messages are vacancy alerts, employer replies, or spam/noise.

tasks.title: Design inbox classifier algorithm
tasks.goal: Define deterministic rules for classifying messages using thread signal, domain signal, and subject keywords.
acceptance: Confidence and ambiguity handling are explicit

tasks.title: Define AI classification contract for ambiguous emails
tasks.goal: Specify when ambiguous messages can escalate to AI classification and what the output contract is.

tasks.title: Implement roleforge/inbox_classifier.py
tasks.goal: Classify new messages as vacancy, employer_reply, or spam with deterministic rules and optional AI fallback.
acceptance: Deterministic classification works for clear cases
Ambiguous cases can fall back to approved AI path

tasks.title: Implement roleforge/jobs/inbox_classify.py
tasks.goal: Run the inbox classifier on new unclassified messages.
acceptance: New unclassified messages can be processed

tasks.title: Implement employer thread matching and employer_threads record creation
tasks.goal: Link employer replies back to the original opportunity context and materialize threads.
acceptance: Employer replies link to an application or vacancy when possible

tasks.title: Implement application state transitions via Telegram actions
tasks.goal: Let the operator move applications through the lifecycle from Telegram.
acceptance: Application states can be updated from Telegram

tasks.title: Implement interview event extraction from employer emails
tasks.goal: Extract interview dates, links, and key details from employer replies into structured interview events.
acceptance: Interview events capture date/time/link when present
Extraction failures remain visible and recoverable

tasks.title: Telegram UX for application update notifications
tasks.goal: Notify the operator when an employer replies or an interview gets scheduled.
acceptance: Employer replies can trigger useful notifications
Interview scheduling triggers a clear alert

tasks.title: Add AI company briefer output to interview_events.notes.ai_briefing
tasks.goal: Generate a concise company briefing for interview preparation when the lifecycle and AI contracts are ready.
acceptance: Company briefings are generated only for relevant events

tasks.title: Add AI prep checklist output to interview_events.notes.prep_checklist
tasks.goal: Generate a bounded prep checklist for scheduled interviews.
acceptance: Prep checklists are generated only for relevant events

tasks.title: Document application lifecycle spec in docs/specs/v5-application-lifecycle.md
tasks.goal: Capture the full v5 workflow in one operator-facing spec.
acceptance: Telegram and AI touchpoints are documented

epics.title: v6 Market Monitoring
epics.summary: Expand beyond email-driven intake with modular active monitoring, starting from HH.ru and preserving the same normalized vacancy pipeline.

tasks.title: Research HH.ru API, rate limits, ToS, and field mapping
tasks.goal: Choose the safest and most maintainable way to monitor HH.ru for new vacancies.
acceptance: Legal and rate-limit tradeoffs are explicit

tasks.title: Design monitor registry model
tasks.goal: Create config/monitors.yaml and roleforge/monitor_registry.py conventions that mirror feed governance.
acceptance: Monitor registry mirrors existing source governance patterns

tasks.title: Implement HH.ru adapter in roleforge/monitors/hh.py
tasks.goal: Fetch, paginate, and map HH.ru vacancies into the standard candidate shape.
acceptance: HH.ru items map to candidate shape
Adapter handles paging/rate limits within chosen approach

tasks.title: Implement roleforge/jobs/monitor_poll.py
tasks.goal: Read the monitor registry, run enabled monitors, and persist results through the same normalize/dedup path.
acceptance: Results go through the canonical pipeline

tasks.title: Add MONITOR_INTAKE_ENABLED kill-switch and per-monitor enabled flag
tasks.goal: Keep market monitoring reversible and easy to shut off independently of Gmail and feeds.

tasks.title: Add salary_structured JSONB to vacancies
tasks.goal: Model structured salary only if the product decision says salary-aware filtering is worth the added complexity.
acceptance: If approved, target JSONB shape is explicit
If deferred, the deferral is documented

tasks.title: Extend scoring to use salary_structured when present
tasks.goal: Use structured salary in hard filters or scoring once salary modeling is approved.
acceptance: Profiles can use salary filters if configured

tasks.title: Document HH.ru ToS acceptance and rate limit policy in docs/architecture.md
tasks.goal: Capture the legal and operational envelope of HH.ru monitoring.

tasks.title: Write docs/specs/v6-market-monitoring.md
tasks.goal: Capture market monitoring workflow, governance, and rollout rules in one spec.
acceptance: Legal and rate-limit policy is documented

epics.title: v7 Web UI
epics.summary: Add a pragmatic single-operator web console for monitoring, analytics, and controlled configuration edits without replacing Telegram.

tasks.title: Define web UI scope and write docs/specs/v7-web-ui.md
tasks.goal: Decide what remains Telegram-first and what deserves a web UI in the first operator console release.
acceptance: Read-only vs editable sections are explicit
human_should_verify: Whether the scope feels truly valuable

tasks.title: Add FastAPI + Jinja2 + HTMX scaffold in roleforge/web/
tasks.goal: Create the minimal web foundation for an internal operator console.
acceptance: Web app can run locally and in hosted runtime
No existing CLI or Telegram path breaks

tasks.title: Implement auth middleware with Bearer token from env or keyring
tasks.goal: Protect the operator console with the simplest acceptable auth path for a single-operator internal tool.
acceptance: Bearer auth works from env or keyring

tasks.title: Implement analytics dashboard
tasks.goal: Expose match counts, score distribution, and funnel views from one page.
acceptance: Dashboard shows match counts by profile and time window

tasks.title: Implement queue browser with bulk state update
tasks.goal: Let the operator browse and update the review queue from the web console without replacing Telegram as the fast path.
acceptance: Queue can be browsed and filtered
Web actions preserve the same state model as Telegram

tasks.title: Implement profile editor for profiles.config JSON
tasks.goal: Allow controlled editing of profile config fields such as keywords, filters, and delivery_mode.
acceptance: profiles.config can be inspected and updated safely

tasks.title: Implement system health panel
tasks.goal: Expose recent job_runs and runtime health indicators in one operator view.

tasks.title: Implement application workspace timeline view
tasks.goal: Expose applications, employer threads, and interviews chronologically in the web console.
acceptance: Applications and interviews can be browsed chronologically
Related vacancy and employer context is visible
Workspace aligns with lifecycle state model

tasks.title: Implement source management view for feeds and monitors
tasks.goal: Expose enable and disable controls for feeds and monitors from the web console.

epics.title: V4+ Observability And Infrastructure
epics.summary: Strengthen logging, failure escalation, and cost governance so the next phases remain observable and safe to operate.

tasks.title: Add JSON structured logging to stdout
tasks.goal: Make logs machine-readable before the number of jobs and sources grows further.

tasks.title: Add consecutive-failure admin alert
tasks.goal: Escalate repeated failures to the operator before they become silent data loss.
acceptance: Jobs failing multiple times notify the admin chat
Transient blips do not spam admin

tasks.title: Add ai_cost_usd to job_runs.summary documentation
tasks.goal: Keep cost accounting explicit as AI enrichment grows.

tasks.title: Document monthly cost report query in docs/specs/cost-governance.md
tasks.goal: Make recurring cost and operations review executable with current tooling.
